channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: 007 First Light Switch 2 Version Delayed to "Later This Summer"
item.content:encoded:
Developer and publisher IO Interactive has announced that it's delaying the Switch 2 version of 007 First Light to an unspecified date in summer, although the game is still set for its original release date on other platforms.

Speaking on X, IO Interactive said earlier today that 007 First Light will still be arriving on PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X|S on May 27th, but that it'll now be launching on Switch 2 "later this summer".

IO Interactive didn't specify a reason for the game's delay, but given that it's just affecting the Switch 2 version, it's possible that the studio has run into technical difficulties with porting 007 First Light to a less powerful system in a similar way to the problem the Borderlands 4 team presumably encountered.

Sadly, IO Interactive also doesn't give a new release date for 007 First Light on Switch 2, so if you want to play the game as soon as possible, you'll be better off picking it up on PC or one of the other two consoles it's hitting in May.

If you're not familiar with 007 First Light, it's the next game from Hitman developer IO Interactive, and it's an origin story that revisits the early days of everyone's favorite suave British operative James Bond.

The game will show the events that led up to Bond obtaining his 00 status (the famous "license to kill"), and it'll see iconic Bond characters like Q and Miss Moneypenny making an appearance, as well as new faces.

007 First Light will follow on from IO Interactive's disastrous first foray into third-party publishing; the studio opted to publish Build a Rocket Boy's ill-fated MindsEye, which subsequently crashed and burned on arrival, leading IO to reportedly swear off publishing others' games in future.

You'll be able to check out 007 First Light when it launches on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S on May 27th, and on Switch 2 at some point later in the year.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
